ops.title: Non-blocking contract for observability-only operations
ops.body:
Observability-only operations — the serving-drift diagnostic, metrics, logging, journaling — must NEVER crash or block the critical flow they observe. In the watcher retrain lane the critical flow is the promote/reject decision; the diagnostic only watches drift.

Contract:
1. Run the observability call behind a TIGHT exception boundary — catch the specific failure family, not a bare `except`. For the serving diagnostic that is `(tinker.TinkerError, OSError, RuntimeError)`: the whole Tinker/API error family, the sidecar-write `OSError`, and the wrapper's own `RuntimeError`.
2. On failure, RECORD the mishap rather than swallowing it silently: a journaled numeric marker (`diagnostic_failed=1.0` in the journal metrics + registry metadata) plus a one-line reason appended to the verdict as a sub-note (same idiom as the `; watch kickstart ok/skipped` note).
3. Return so the gate outcome proceeds untouched.

Pattern: `cc_steer/retrain/watcher.py::_safe_serving_diagnostic` wraps the raising `_serving_diagnostic`, mirroring `cc_steer/journal.py::Journal._run` (tight catch `(OSError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired)`, degrade to a sentinel, never crash on bookkeeping).

Rationale: under eval-what-you-serve a genuine conversion bug already surfaces as a served AUC at chance (the floor rejects it), so the diagnostic never needs to block. A Tinker timeout or a full disk on the sidecar write must not turn a clean promote into an unjournaled crash — that would be the exact opposite of "non-blocking observability".
ops.when: Before modifying the serving diagnostic, retrain error boundaries, or designing any observability/monitoring that must not block a critical flow (promotion, gating, persistence)
